design_philosophy.core_loop: Select a 3-character team and defeat opposing teams in sequential matches, culminating in boss fights against Saisyu Kusanagi and Omega Rugal
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Team composition strategy balanced against individual character mastery; rival dynamics introduced through Iori Yagami's emergence as Kyo's foil
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Commanding a fighting team through an international tournament while uncovering a conspiracy involving brainwashing and demonic power (Orochi)
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Team-based fighting game structure (3v3) rather than 1v1 isolation; introduced Team Edit feature allowing custom roster creation, signaling design belief that player agency in team construction enhances competitive depth; introduced narrative continuity (Orochi Saga) into arcade fighting game genre, blending tournament spectacle with serialized storytelling

difficulty.lives_system: Team-based elimination: player loses when all three team members are defeated
difficulty.continues_system: Arcade continues available (specific mechanics not documented)
difficulty.checkpointing: No checkpointing within tournament bracket; arcade ladder progression preserved per run
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High: Team Edit feature allows players to construct teams matching their skill level and playstyle; character roster diversity enables difficulty modulation through team selection

progression.unlock_mechanism: Defeat tournament teams sequentially to access mid-boss (Saisyu Kusanagi) and final boss (Omega Rugal); Team Edit mode unlocks custom team creation, expanding roster accessibility
replayability_hooks: 24-character roster enables numerous team composition experiments
Team Edit feature supports competitive team building optimization
Rival Team storyline (Iori introduction) creates narrative replay incentive
Boss fight against Omega Rugal (enhanced form) requires pattern learning and counter-strategy

world_building_techniques: Character roster drawn from prior SNK fighting game universes (Fatal Fury, Art of Fighting, Ikari Warriors) creating narrative continuity
Tournament premise as framing device for character encounters
Rival Team introduction as narrative twist—team invitation theft and substitution creates story tension
Saisyu Kusanagi brainwashing reveal adds conspiracy layer to tournament structure
Rugal's survival and Orochi power usage establishes long-form antagonist arc (Orochi Saga continuation)

boss_design.philosophy: Two-tier boss progression: mid-boss emphasizes story revelation (Saisyu brainwashing), final boss tests mastered mechanics through enhanced stat variant (Omega Rugal as power escalation)
notable_bosses: Saisyu Kusanagi (mid-boss): Story-focused encounter revealing brainwashing plot, first playable appearance
Omega Rugal (final boss): Enhanced version of KOF '94 final boss, represents mechanical and narrative culmination, powered by Orochi demon force

faction_indicators: Team affiliation determines narrative voice (Hero Team, Fatal Fury Team, Art of Fighting Team, Ikari Warriors Team, Psycho Soldier Team, Korea Team, Women Fighters Team, Rivals Team)
Iori Yagami's Rivals Team positioned as antagonistic faction opposing Hero Team's Kyo
Character crossover from multiple fighting game universes suggests interconnected SNK fighting multiverse
danger_signals: Rugal Bernstein's resurrection after presumed death indicates hidden threat persistence
Saisyu Kusanagi's brainwashing reveals external control mechanisms and infiltration capabilities
Orochi demon power introduction foreshadows supernatural/dark force elements in narrative arc
Iori Yagami's rival positioning suggests internal conflict within fighting community
environmental_storytelling: Tournament structure as international gathering reveals geopolitical scope (Korea Team, American invitations stolen, etc.)
Team compositions reflect cultural/organizational identities (Women Fighters Team, Korea Team, Psycho Soldier Team)
Invitation theft by Rivals Team demonstrates agency and disruption within established tournament system
